title: Generides (couplet version)
summary: The checked-in CME00043 XML is a repository copy of the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse text derived from Frederick J. Furnivall's Roxburghe Club edition A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides. The encoding selects the edition's dedication leaf, the manuscript-derived prose summary, and the complete 10,086-line couplet romance on printed pp. 1-312; it omits the edition's title page, contents, preface, fragments of the independent stanzaic version, corrigenda, 1866 postscript, glossary, and index. Furnivall states that W. A. Dalziel made the working transcript from the sole continuous witness, now Morgan Library & Museum MS M.876, ff. 103r-152v, and that Furnivall checked the transcript and printed text. A complete scan of the augmented 1865/1866 printed volume is cached. The Morgan publicly exposes only seven selected 600-pixel manuscript images, four of them within Generides; that complete published selection is cached as partial manuscript coverage. No public complete-object facsimile of M.876 was found, but one can be requested for a fee through the Morgan's Imaging & Rights service.
review_notes: The intellectual subject is the anonymous late-fourteenth-century Middle English couplet version of Generides, not the independent stanzaic version, Morgan MS M.876 as a whole, Furnivall's edition, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML.
The manuscript work occupies ff. 103r-152v. Its prose contents rubric begins on f. 103r; Furnivall's first verse milestone places the prologue on f. 103v (his '103b').

The seven Morgan web images are an official selection, not a complete manuscript facsimile. Images 4-7 overlap the work at ff. 113r, 119v, 121v, and 133r; images 1-3 show adjacent Lydgate material at ff. 6r, 59r, and 87r.

Furnivall's title page is dated 1865, while the scanned volume also contains a postscript dated 19 February 1866 and a 1866 Roxburghe Club membership list. Those dates are not composition dates of the medieval romance.
Dalziel's working transcript is provisionally typed manuscript_witness because schema 1.0.0 has no historical editorial-transcript entity type; it is not a second medieval witness.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00043
notes: The file is 660,858 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit and Git blob.
TEMPHEAD documents review on 19 August 2016 by lattaj with responsibility MURP; it does not identify the original keying date.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00043

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The revision entry records review, not initial creation.

notes: The U-M item labels its first front division 'title page', but the encoded text is the edition's dedication to the Roxburghe Club rather than the bibliographic title page.

The encoding includes the prose manuscript summary, prologue, twenty-two chapters, Furnivall's marginal English explanations, manuscript-folio and printed-page milestones, and textual notes through printed p. 312.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides (1865, with 1866 postscript)

date_statements.label: postscript
date_statements.notes: The postscript supplies further fragments of the stanzaic version and postdates the 1865 title page.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides, edited from the unique MS. of John Tollemache, Esq., M.P., of Peckforton Castle, South Cheshire, and Helmingham Hall, Suffolk (Hertford: printed for Henry Hucks Gibbs by Stephen Austin for the Roxburghe Club, 1865; postscript dated 19 February 1866).
bibliographic.title: A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides
bibliographic.publisher: Printed for Henry Hucks Gibbs by Stephen Austin for the Roxburghe Club

notes: The complete couplet text occupies printed pp. 1-312 and is followed by glossary and index; preliminary fragments and the 1866 postscript concern the independent stanzaic version.
Furnivall states that Dalziel copied the text carefully from the manuscript, after which Furnivall read the copy once and the printed text twice.
The scanned copy represents an augmented physical state containing 1866 matter despite its 1865 title page; the exact issue history of that augmentation remains unresolved.

entities.label: W. A. Dalziel's working transcript of Sir Generides from Morgan MS M.876
entities.description: The otherwise untraced working copy that Furnivall says Dalziel made carefully from the manuscript for the 1865 edition.
notes: Typed as manuscript_witness only because the lineage schema has no working-transcript type; this modern editorial intermediary is not a medieval codex or a second textual witness.
No present repository or separate copy of the transcript was found.

holding.notes: Furnivall edited the codex while it belonged to John Tollemache and was preserved at Helmingham Hall; the Morgan later purchased it from A. Rosenthal.

physical_description.format: bound codex; two columns; 60 lines per page through f. 152 and 52 lines on f. 153
notes: The codex contains an incomplete copy of Lydgate's Troy Book at ff. 1r-102v, Sir Generides at ff. 103r-152v, and a thirteenth-century Psalm-commentary leaf at f. 153r.
The Morgan records two painted miniatures and five sketched but unpainted miniatures; illumination is unfinished.
The current binding is eighteenth-century tan sprinkled calf with Tollemache-Dysart arms; the complete binding and endpapers are not exposed in the public seven-image selection.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides
entities.reproduction_format: 380-page text PDF with public item metadata and page-number map
notes: The cached PDF is complete for the scanned bound volume and retains the 1866 postscript, glossary, index, preliminary leaves, and copy-specific matter rather than only the encoded work.

entities.label: Morgan MS M.876 official seven-image selection
entities.reproduction_format: Seven public low-resolution color JPEGs, each 600 pixels high, assembled from the official image list
notes: This is complete only for the provider's seven-image selection, not for the 153-leaf codex or even for the work at ff. 103r-152v.
Visual inspection confirms color captures of both painted and unpainted leaves, but the 600-pixel access files are reference derivatives rather than publication-quality images.

entities.label: Morgan MS M.876 curatorial description and additional bibliography
entities.reproduction_format: Three-page scanned curatorial-description PDF and one-page additional-bibliography PDF
notes: The Morgan cautions that historical curatorial descriptions can contain superseded scholarship; the current object catalog controls current shelfmark, extent, contents, and access.

agents.name: W. A. Dalziel
notes: Named by initials and surname in Furnivall's preface as the manuscript transcriber; no fuller identity is inferred here.

scope.notes: Identifiers, structure, content, and revision data align; byte identity with an upstream downloadable source package has not been tested.

subject.description: dedication leaf, manuscript summary, prologue, and twenty-two chapters represented in the U-M text

object.description: edition dedication leaf and complete edited couplet romance

scope.notes: The bibliographic title page, contents, preface, independent stanzaic-version fragments, corrigenda, 1866 postscript, glossary, and index are outside the encoding.
Furnivall reports checking the transcript and printed text; the edition is edited rather than a photographic or diplomatic reproduction.

subject.description: preface, physical description, editorial conventions, and edited text

object.description: Sir Generides and its manuscript presentation

scope.notes: Furnivall retained the manuscript in his possession, describes its rubrication and physical features, and thanks Tollemache for the loan; the material text-setting intermediary remains Dalziel's transcript.

subject.description: complete 380-page scan of the bound volume

object.description: seven discontiguous manuscript leaves, four within Generides

scope.notes: The selection does not expose the complete codex, binding, endleaves, blanks, adjacent text leaves, or most Generides leaves.

access.access_method: public official selection of seven low-resolution images

local_copies.label: Morgan Library MS M.876 official seven-image selection
notes: Complete capture of the seven low-resolution images selected by the Morgan website on 2026-07-12; not a complete codex facsimile.

Three images (ff. 6r, 59r, 87r) precede Sir Generides; four images (ff. 113r, 119v, 121v, 133r) fall within it. No images are exposed for most of the work at ff. 103r-152v, binding, endleaves, blanks, adjacent text leaves, or other surfaces.
The Morgan terms permit download for personal, educational, or noncommercial use; publication and commercial use require Imaging & Rights authorization.
ZIP contains the normalized source URL list, an exact-source inventory, and all seven provider-resource JPEGs; each source image is 600 pixels high.

work_portion.label: Generides (couplet version)
locators: selected images overlapping the work: bundle images 4-7, ff. 113r, 119v, 121v, and 133r
notes: This is a discontiguous four-image sample within the work, not continuous coverage of ff. 103r-152v.
The Morgan's manuscript list distinguishes 'Images' from 'Digital Facsimile'; M.876 is listed only with Images.

access.access_method: dated audit of exact object record, manuscript list, selected-image inventory, and digital-facsimile classification
notes: Only seven selected folio images were exposed. No publicly obtainable complete sequence covering binding exteriors, spine, endpapers, endleaves, blanks, all 153 manuscript leaves, adjacent works, and other physical surfaces was found.

access.access_method: online Imaging & Rights request for existing or newly approved digital photography; request the entire physical object and state research and publication uses separately
access.format: digital images; new photography subject to preservation approval
notes: A complete-object request should explicitly include both covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all endpapers and endleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf including adjacent Lydgate and Psalm-commentary material, and edge views if offered; identify Generides separately as ff. 103r-152v.
The Morgan quotes each order and does not publish a fixed item price; publication and commercial permissions are separate from research delivery.

access.access_method: reader application and confirmed appointment, subject to preservation and repository approval
Furnivall records the transcript's use but supplies no holding or later history
notes: This access uncertainty concerns Dalziel's editorial working copy, not Morgan MS M.876.

rights.component: Underlying late-medieval Middle English textual content
rights.basis: The medieval text predates modern copyright; this assertion does not extend to manuscript photography, modern transcription, metadata, or provider contracts.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1865 edited text, preface, notes, arrangement, glossary, index, and 1866 postscript
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1865-1866 and the U-M item describes its materials as public domain with a No Copyright-United States statement.

rights.component: U-M CME00043 item and encoded textual materials
rights.basis: The official item page says the materials are in the public domain and displays the RightsStatements.org No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: The statement is jurisdiction-specific and does not license Morgan manuscript images.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and item presentation
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain, but the exact Internet Archive metadata supplies no standardized license or rights URI for this scan derivative.
notes: Public download availability is not treated as an independent provider copyright dedication.

rights.component: Seven low-resolution Morgan manuscript photographs and their web presentation
rights.jurisdiction: United States and any jurisdiction specified by the Morgan
rights.basis: The Morgan terms state that site content is copyrighted, allow unaltered image downloads only for personal, educational, or noncommercial use with full credit, and require Imaging & Rights contact for publication or commercial use.
notes: The Git-ignored local cache records the permitted private research download; it is not authorization to redistribute the images in Git, a public web build, a print-on-demand product, or another commercial publication.
The Morgan says it will not grant reproduction or commercial-use permission for the low-resolution downloadable images; publication-quality files must be requested separately.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and any jurisdiction specified by the Morgan
rights.basis: The Morgan's website copyright notice applies; public access and private research caching do not establish redistribution permission.
notes: The PDFs remain in the ignored research cache and should not be shipped as public corpus assets without permission.

rights.component: Existing or newly commissioned complete-object manuscript photography
rights.jurisdiction: United States and any jurisdiction specified in supplied terms
rights.basis: The Morgan sells files for research and publication purposes, requires detailed intended-use information, and forbids unauthorized duplication or dissemination of its photographic material.
notes: Any delivery terms must be checked separately for private retention, automated processing, quotation, scholarly publication, Git redistribution, and commercial print-on-demand use.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete M.876 codex contains Lydgate's Troy Book, Sir Generides, and a Psalm-commentary leaf.
editorial_practices.representation: Dalziel transcribed and Furnivall printed Sir Generides at ff. 103r-152v as a separate edited work.
editorial_practices.description: The edition's continuous couplet text is mediated by Dalziel's working transcript rather than reproducing the whole codex.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are printed in italics; Furnivall disregards ordinary non-contraction strokes and tails except specified lined letters.
editorial_practices.description: Italics in ordinary words generally represent editorial expansion, not manuscript emphasis.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Red names, rubricated headings, later black-ink name insertions, and unfilled initials
editorial_practices.representation: Names genuinely written in red are italicized; rubricated chapter or illustration headings use ordinary type; later black-ink insertions are generally ignored.
editorial_practices.description: Printed typography selectively signals manuscript rubrication and should not be read as a direct color facsimile.
editorial_practices.scope: Edited couplet text and headings on pp. 1-312

editorial_practices.source_feature: Sparse manuscript metrical points, blanks, damage, and uncertain readings
editorial_practices.representation: Manuscript metrical points are printed in parentheses; editorially supplied names and damaged readings appear in brackets, with corrections and manuscript notes in the apparatus.
editorial_practices.description: Punctuation, brackets, and supplied text are editorial signals rather than literal manuscript characters.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete printed volume contains bibliographic and club preliminaries, preface, stanzaic-version fragments, corrigenda, postscript, couplet text, glossary, and index.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML retains the dedication leaf and complete couplet romance with edition notes but omits the rest of the volume.
editorial_practices.description: CME00043 is an edition-derived textual selection, not a digital facsimile or a complete encoding of the 1865/1866 volume.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00043.xml
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG; TEXT/@LANG; FRONT; BODY; opening and closing L elements; PB and manuscript-folio MILESTONE elements
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for identifiers, Middle English content, the edition dedication leaf, manuscript summary, complete couplet text, editorial notes, printed-page and manuscript-folio mapping, and documented 2016 review.

evidence.citation: XPath and structural inspection of CME00043.xml
evidence.locator: DIV1, HEAD, L, LG, MILESTONE, NOTE, P, PB; ancestor-sensitive counts
evidence.summary: Finds twenty-five DIV1 elements overall: two front divisions plus a prologue and twenty-two numbered chapters; all 10,086 L elements are direct verse lines in twenty-three LG elements and none is nested in NOTE. The file also has 313 PB, 2,914 MILESTONE, 2,449 NOTE, and 28 P elements.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00043
evidence.locator: item title, publication, Rights/Permissions, contents, and full-text action
evidence.summary: Supplies the public item identity, Furnivall bibliographic source, public electronic-text route, contents structure, public-domain statement, and No Copyright-United States rights label.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides (Hertford, 1865)
evidence.locator: title page; dedication; contents; printed pp. 1-334
evidence.summary: Primary bibliographic and textual evidence for title, editor, publisher, publication date and place, dedication, complete couplet text on pp. 1-312, glossary, index, and relation to the Tollemache manuscript.

evidence.citation: Furnivall's preface to A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides
evidence.locator: pp. xiii-xxiv, especially pp. xx-xxiv
evidence.summary: States the manuscript's then ownership, date and contents, identifies M.876 ff. 103-152v, records rubrication and expansion conventions, describes meter and dialect cautiously, names W. A. Dalziel as transcriber, and records Furnivall's checking of copy and print.

evidence.citation: Postscript to Furnivall's A Royal Historie of the Excellent Knight Generides
evidence.locator: pp. xxxiii*-xxxvi*, dated 19 February 1866
evidence.summary: Documents the post-title-page addition of further fragments from the independent stanzaic version and Wright's rediscovery of Trinity College MS O.5.2.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 3647, A royal historie of the excellent knight Generides
evidence.locator: uniform title, created/published statement, physical description, notes
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1865 Hertford imprint, xxxvi plus 334-page extent, editor statement, binder's title, Roxburghe Club number, and 1866 postscript note.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item royalhistorieofe00roxbrich
evidence.locator: metadata, files, PDF, and page-number map
evidence.summary: Verifies the exact public scan, source institution, direct file routes, 380-page PDF, derivative metadata, and absence of a standardized license or rights field in the exact item metadata.

evidence.citation: Local inspection of cached Internet Archive Furnivall files
evidence.locator: PDF pages 1-380; title and dedication; preliminaries; printed pp. 1-334; postscript; file metadata and page map
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete readable scan of the bound printed volume; maps the XML's dedication to PDF p. 9 and its manuscript summary plus edited romance to PDF pp. 41-352; verifies file signatures, byte counts, and SHA-256 values for the PDF and companion JSON files.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.876
evidence.locator: accession, created, binding, description, provenance, notes, contents, script, and language
evidence.summary: Current institutional evidence for shelfmark, circa 1400-1450 date, vellum support, 153-leaf extent, dimensions, binding, provenance, contents, unfinished illumination, and Sir Generides at ff. 103r-152v.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.876 curatorial description
evidence.locator: three-page curatorial description
evidence.summary: Supports codex extent and contents, 1440-1450 execution, script, decoration, provenance, binding, relation of the two English Generides versions, and historical bibliography, subject to the Morgan's caution about superseded description layers.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.876 additional bibliography
evidence.locator: one-page bibliography
evidence.summary: Provides a repository-maintained starting bibliography for M.876, including Meale, Driver, and Whetter.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.876 official image selection
evidence.locator: seven thumbnails and page records; Generides images 4-7
evidence.summary: Enumerates exactly seven selected folio images at ff. 6r, 59r, 87r, 113r, 119v, 121v, and 133r and identifies the last four as Generides scenes; provides no full-codex sequence.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the cached Morgan MS M.876 official seven-image selection
evidence.locator: source-list.json, inventory.json, and images/000001.jpg-images/000007.jpg
evidence.summary: Verifies a clean ZIP containing all seven official 600-pixel-high JPEG resources, exact page and image URLs, checksums, and labels; visual inspection confirms color capture of painted f. 121v and unpainted f. 113r. Coverage remains partial for the manuscript.

evidence.citation: Morgan Medieval & Renaissance Manuscripts Online list
evidence.locator: MS M.876 row and Digital Facsimile versus Images labels
evidence.summary: Lists M.876 with 'Images' rather than the separate 'Digital Facsimile' access category, supporting the dated finding that the seven selected images are not a complete facsimile.

evidence.locator: Copyright Notice and Image use
evidence.summary: States that website content is copyrighted; permits unaltered image downloads for personal, educational, or noncommercial use with full credit; and directs publication or commercial users to Imaging & Rights.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Imaging & Rights
evidence.locator: photography formats, request method, publication, payment, timing, and policy
evidence.summary: Documents fee-based digital image supply for research and publication, required request information, processing windows, preservation review for new photography, and the prohibition on unauthorized duplication or dissemination.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Application for Photography
evidence.locator: contact, photography type, item description, publication, and rights fields
evidence.summary: Provides the current online order route for digital photography and separately asks whether images are intended for publication, along with publisher, print-run, language, and distribution details.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Sherman Fairchild Reading Room
evidence.locator: reader application, material selection, appointment confirmation, and contact
evidence.summary: Documents onsite access by advance reader application and confirmed appointment and supplies the Reading Room contact address.

evidence.citation: Laurent Brun, 'Generides (version en couplets)', ARLIMA
evidence.locator: date, language, genre, form, incipit, explicit, manuscript, edition, DIMEV 111
evidence.summary: Specialist bibliographic evidence distinguishing the couplet version from the stanzaic version, dating it to the late fourteenth century, counting 10,086 rhyming-couplet lines, identifying M.876 ff. 103r-152v as its sole manuscript, and citing Furnivall's edition.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, 'Generides'
evidence.locator: date, place, form, plot, manuscripts, early prints, and modern editions
evidence.summary: University of York specialist evidence for late-fourteenth-century composition in the Midlands, the two long Middle English forms, romance subjects, witness census, distinction between the M.876 couplet text and Trinity O.5.2 stanzaic text, and Furnivall's source manuscript.

open_questions.question: Can the Morgan supply a retainable complete physical-object facsimile of MS M.876 beyond its seven public selected images?
next_steps: Submit the Morgan photography form for the entire object, explicitly requesting both covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all endpapers and endleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf and adjacent work, and edge views if available.
Map Generides separately as ff. 103r-152v after receipt and preserve the Morgan's exact sequence and filenames.
Obtain written terms distinguishing private research retention, automated analysis, scholarly quotation, public Git redistribution, electronic publication, and commercial print-on-demand use.
Keep the seven-image cache private and coverage=partial unless a provider-authorized complete sequence is actually delivered.

open_questions.question: What historical source chain lies behind the couplet version's prologue claim that a French rhyme was rendered into Latin at Hertford before the English narration?
next_steps: Compare the prologue and both English versions with current source scholarship rather than treating its narrative of transmission as a surviving-document inventory.
Search for evidence of the alleged French and Latin texts, while preserving the current fact that no exact source copy is identified.
Distinguish shared narrative ancestry from direct translation and distinguish the abstract source text from any hypothetical physical exemplar.

open_questions.question: Does W. A. Dalziel's working transcript survive, and can its role in typesetting and Furnivall's checking be reconstructed more exactly?
next_steps: Search Furnivall papers, Roxburghe Club archives, Gibbs and Tollemache correspondence, and printer records for the transcript or proof sheets.
Do not collapse the edition directly into the manuscript while the preface explicitly identifies this material intermediary.
Preserve the initials-only identification of Dalziel unless documentary evidence supplies a full name.

open_questions.question: How was the 19 February 1866 postscript distributed in relation to copies bearing the 1865 title page and Christmas 1865 dedication?
next_steps: Compare multiple institutional copies for cancellanda, separately issued sheets, binding order, and presence or absence of the postscript and 1866 member list.
Consult Roxburghe Club and printer records before calling the scanned state a formal revised edition or reissue.
Keep the XML derivation scoped to the dedication and pp. 1-312, which do not depend on resolving the postscript's issue history.

open_questions.question: What initial keying method, source copy, correction history, and source-file reuse terms apply to U-M CME00043?
next_steps: Request item-level production metadata and any downloadable source package from U-M.
Keep the 2016 TEMPHEAD entry scoped to review rather than initial creation.
Compare XML against the cached printed scan and manuscript images before treating typographic differences as intentional normalization.
